title: Agropyron repens lusus semiverticillatum
taxonomy: Agropyron repens lusus semiverticillatum is a variant of the creeping bentgrass or blackgrass (Agropyron repens) that exhibits a unique, semiverticillate (partially whorled) inflorescence. This unusual growth habit is characteristic of the lusus semiverticillatum variant and is likely the result of genetic mutation. As a variant of the more common Agropyron repens, it shares many of the same characteristics, such as its ability to form dense mats and its prolific seed production. The Agropyron repens lusus semiverticillatum is a member of the Poaceae family and is closely related to other grasses in this family. Further research is necessary to fully understand the classification and taxonomic status of this variant.
